eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to Quorum Minimum Correctness Configuration (4 IBFT validator nodes)

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Conservative technology-level estimate for Quorum IBFT with 4 validators. Real performance varies with block parameters, hardware, network conditions and workload composition.
ps_throughput.source: GoQuorum IBFT consensus documentation and PoA comparison

eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: GoQuorum free gas network documentation; gas price is zero by default

eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework baseline storage assumption for Quorum MCC with full state replication across 4 validators

parallel_execution.motivation: Independent transactions can be processed concurrently at execution level, even though final ordering remains global.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: The MCC assumes a single globally replicated ledger across all validator nodes and excludes privacy-layer partitioning.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: IBFT requires global block ordering and serialized ledger progression.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: IBFT requires coordinated validator communication and voting, which introduces structural coordination overhead rather than elastic coordination.

ps_scalability.source: GoQuorum IBFT architecture and global consensus model

append_only_structural_history.motivation: Quorum maintains blockchain history as an append-only sequence of blocks.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Blocks and transactions are linked through standard Ethereum-style cryptographic hashing and block references.
cryptographic_linkage_between_records.source: GoQuorum genesis and blockchain structure documentation

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions are signed using Ethereum account cryptography before acceptance into the ledger.
cryptographic_write_authentication.source: GoQuorum network and chain ID documentation

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Each validator maintains the replicated blockchain state and can independently verify ledger integrity under the common protocol rules.

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: IBFT provides immediate finality; valid committed blocks are finalized without fork competition.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Block commitment requires validator agreement under the IBFT protocol rather than unilateral acceptance.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: No single validator can unilaterally reorganize finalized chain history in the IBFT model.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.configuration_note: Score refers strictly to the native 4-validator IBFT model and does not include geographic redundancy or enterprise HA extensions.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Write progression does not depend on a single database writer; validator leadership rotates within the IBFT protocol.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: The network can continue processing blocks as long as the protocol quorum is maintained.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: IBFT is designed to continue after validator failures within tolerance bounds through protocol rounds and validator coordination.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: All validator nodes replicate the blockchain state, so write capability survives single-node loss in the 4-validator native model.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.source: GoQuorum IBFT quorum-based write continuation model

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: Ledger progression and validator-set changes are not structurally unilateral under IBFT validator governance.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Validation is structurally multi-validator under the IBFT protocol.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Quorum is permissioned; validator admission is governed by existing network participants and permissioning rules.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The validator set is explicitly governed and managed rather than open and permissionless.
absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.source: GoQuorum add nodes / validator management documentation

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: GoQuorum supports node and account permissioning with differentiated roles and access controls.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: Enhanced permissioning supports organization, account and role management, including approval workflows.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: Identity and access governance remain consortium-administered rather than structurally independent from network authorities.

ags_collusion_resilience.configuration_note: Specific to the native 4-validator IBFT model; score reflects tolerance of one faulty validator and the need for compromise beyond a single node.

non_unilateral_compromise_possible.motivation: A single validator is insufficient to compromise IBFT consensus in the 4-validator model.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Compromise requires control beyond one validator and therefore exceeds unilateral failure.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: The framework does not classify the 4-validator IBFT case as requiring a supermajority-compromise threshold at the highest resilience level.

ags_collusion_resilience.source: GoQuorum IBFT fault tolerance properties operationalized in framework form
